name: Ordinary Rasam
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons red gram dal (pigeon peas, toor dal), picked over & rinsed
a lemon-sized piece of tamarind pulp
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the red gram dal well and drain. Place in a heavy saucepan. Cover with 1 cup water and bring to a boil.
2. When boiling, cover pan with a lid, leaving slightly ajar. Lower the heat and simmer dal gently for 1 1/2 hours. Stir several times during the last 30 minutes of cooking. Set dal aside without draining.
3. Dice the tomatoes. Place in a heavy saucepan with the tamarind pulp, asafoetida powder, rasam powder, and salt to taste. Add 1 cup water.
4. Crush the contents of the pan together. Simmer for about 15 minutes.
5. Add the undrained cooked dal and the remaining 1 1/2 cups water to the rasam. Bring to a boil.
6. For tempering, heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a heavy frying pan or skillet. Add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds, halved red chili, and a few curry leaves.
7. When the mustard seeds splutter, add this mixture to the rasam.
8. Garnish the rasam with chopped coriander leaves. Serve hot.
